'''Frankie Halton''' (born 18 June 1996) is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland international]] [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played at club level for the [[Swinton Lions]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Born in [[Manchester]], Halton started his rugby league career in the reserve team at the [[Leigh Centurions]]. When the reserve team was discontinued, he was loaned out to the [[North Wales Crusaders]], but Halton chose to return to amateur rugby instead, and spent the next few seasons with [[Leigh Miners Rangers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Why Hull KR new boy Frankie Halton is benefiting from being in a full-time environment |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/why-hull-kr-new-boy-frankie-halton-is-benefiting-from-being-in-a-full-time-environment-3522190 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=19 July 2022 |date=11 January 2022}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Swinton Lions===
Halton returned to the professional game in 2019, signing for [[Swinton Lions]].

===Featherstone Rovers===
In September 2020, Halton signed a two-year contract with [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Featherstone Rovers snap up 'young, hungry' Lion Frankie Halton on two-year contract |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-snap-up-young-hungry-lion-frankie-halton-on-two-year-contract-2958619 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=19 July 2022 |date=1 September 2020}}</ref>

He reached the [[2021 RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] final with Featherstone, but did not play in the final as he was required to self-isolate due to [[COVID-19]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Star set to miss Wembley final due to COVID |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/star-set-to-miss-wembley-final-due-to-covid-41079/ |publisher=Serious About Rugby League |access-date=19 July 2022 |date=14 July 2021}}</ref>

His final game for the club was in the [[Million Pound Game]] against [[Toulouse Olympique|Toulouse]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Million Pound Game: 34-12 - Toulouse beat Featherstone to win promotion to Super League |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58824126 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=19 July 2022 |date=10 October 2021}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In July 2021, Halton signed a two-year contract with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] beginning at the start of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Frankie Halton: Hull KR sign Featherstone forward on two-year deal from 2022 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57715042 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=19 July 2022 |date=4 July 2021}}</ref>

In July 2022, Halton extended his contract with the club until the end of the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Frankie Halton: Hull KR second-row signs two-year contract extension |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62076404 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=19 July 2022 |date=7 July 2022}}</ref>
On 17 May 2023, Halton signed a two-and-a-half-year deal to join the newly promoted [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] side effective immediately.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65621835#:~:text=Leigh%20Leopards%20have%20signed%20second,release%20him%20from%20his%20contract./|title=Frankie Halton: Leigh Leopards sign second row after Hull KR release|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Leigh===
Halton made his debut for Leigh in a 30–12 win against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], but suffered a torn pectoral injury which ruled him out for an extended period.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/frankie-halton-leigh-hull-kr-8475902|title=Ex-Hull KR man Frankie Halton injured in Leigh debut as Adrian Lam confirms setback|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|date=28 May 2023 }}</ref>
Halton played 26 games for Leigh in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish fifth on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12204/13227859/super-league-semi-finals-live-wigan-face-leigh-for-grand-final-spot/|title=Super League semi-finals: Wigan Warriors cruise past Leigh Leopards- live commentary and video clips|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
